meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Idea Validation Checklist for Founders: Stop Quitting SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Break the cycle of unfinished projects. Use this intermediate guide to validate your SaaS idea using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure market demand.
content.answer_block: You fail to finish because you build features, not solutions. Stop coding until you have validated demand. Use a lean MVP approach to test market interest, leverage AI agents for rapid iteration, and kill ideas that don't generate revenue signals within 14 days. Stop guessing and start validating.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfect' codebases that never saw a single paying user. You suffer from the 'Infinite Build Loop'—a seductive trap where tweaking your tech stack feels like progress, but it’s actually fear disguised as productivity. You are not building a product; you are building an expensive hobby.

Real founders don't fall in love with their features; they fall in love with the problem. To break this cycle, we shift from 'developer mode' to 'architect mode.' By combining the structural power of Antigravity with the autonomous execution of Claude Code, we can validate your business model faster than you can write a single line of boilerplate.

steps.title: Define the Value Prop Hypothesis
steps.description: Articulate the specific pain point and the 'must-have' solution. If you cannot explain your product in one sentence, you cannot build it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Review this core value proposition and challenge its assumptions. Identify the top 3 risks that would prevent users from paying for this solution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze my README.md for product-market fit weaknesses and output a risk assessment matrix.'

steps.title: Build a Landing Page Feedback Loop
steps.description: Create a high-converting landing page to collect email signups before writing backend code. This measures intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-conversion landing page structure using Tailwind CSS and Framer Motion that focuses on solving [User Problem] for [Target Audience]. Include a clear CTA.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a landing page component in /src/pages/index.tsx based on the validated value proposition.'

steps.title: Automate Data Collection
steps.description: Set up a lightweight backend to capture leads and log interactions, ensuring you have a data-driven path to launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema for a Supabase-backed lead collection system. Include fields for user pain points and source attribution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup Supabase client and create a lead capture function in /lib/api.ts'

steps.title: Simulate User Workflows
steps.description: Before full development, mock the core user experience to see if the solution actually solves the problem in a frictionless way.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a user journey map for the 'Happy Path' of this SaaS. Identify where friction occurs and suggest AI-driven automations to remove it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate an E2E test script in Playwright that simulates the core user sign-up and onboarding flow.'

steps.title: Iterate via Revenue Signals
steps.description: Analyze your conversion data. If users aren't signing up, pivot the messaging or the feature set immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the conversion data provided. Suggest three pivots for the landing page messaging to increase sign-up rates by 20%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the landing page hero section based on the pivot strategy provided by Antigravity.'

pro_tips: Never build an auth system from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 40 hours of engineering time.
If you haven't talked to 10 potential customers, you aren't building a SaaS, you're building a hallucination.
Use 'Feature Flags' to ship partial code; if a feature isn't getting used, turn it off without deleting the codebase.
Optimize for 'Time-to-First-Value' (TTFV) over feature density. Users pay for speed, not complexity.
Treat your code as disposable assets. If the market rejects the idea, kill the codebase and pivot within 48 hours.
content.next_steps: Deploy your landing page, drive traffic via cold outreach, and analyze the conversion rate. If you hit < 5% conversion, iterate the value prop before writing another line of code.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum. Start building for a market that is waiting for your solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
